## Source: amazon-inspector (15ae01d96f3589f6660b2aa5f459595c9346ab885eda35196dae6252775f986a)
On `npm install`, this package's postinstall hook performs two unsafe install-time actions. First, dist/postinstall.js spawns a detached `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com` with `stdio:'ignore'` — silently installing an unrelated third-party AI CLI globally on the installer's machine without consent or version pin. Second, dist/service-installer.js executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` (unpinned, mutable tag), then dist/daemon-manager.js invokes the resulting binary with elevated privileges via PowerShell `Start-Process … -Verb RunAs`, `pkexec`, `sudo`, or `osascript` admin to register a persistent systemd/launchd/Windows service that auto-starts at boot. Because both fetches are unpinned `@latest` against mutable registries, the author (or anyone who later compromises either package) can ship arbitrary code that runs with root/Administrator privileges on every installer's machine simply by publishing a new version of `claw-subagent-service` or `opencode-ai`. Additionally, on first plugin load dist/auto-register.js POSTs the host's MAC address and hostname to https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/register. The IM-registration beacon is consistent with the package's stated purpose, but the silent global install of an unrelated package and the unpinned privileged service registration are not.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
